_comment: Tests Speeding Health's key disambiguation gates: unqualified 'massage' must ask Glen vs Rachel; Clinical Therapy returning must ask sub-type; gender gate must fire before duration for Rachel's massage.

scenarios.name: Unqualified massage — must ask Glen or Rachel

history.content: I'd like to book a massage.

scenarios.expected_behavior: PASS if agent asks 'Were you after Sports Massage with Glen or Massage with Rachel?' as a spoken-only turn. FAIL if agent routes to either practitioner directly, asks a duration question, or asks any other question before disambiguating practitioner.

scenarios.name: Clinical Therapy returning — must ask sub-type before routing

history.content: I'd like to book a clinical therapy session.
Have you had clinical therapy with us before?

scenarios.expected_behavior: PASS if agent asks 'Were you after an ongoing sports therapy session or a Report of Findings?' as a spoken-only turn. FAIL if agent routes to either sub-type without asking, or calls confirm_service silently.

scenarios.name: Rachel massage gender gate fires before duration question

history.content: I'd like a massage with Rachel please.

scenarios.expected_behavior: PASS if agent asks the gender gate question ('Just so you know, Rachel's massage is for female clients — is that okay?') before asking any duration question. FAIL if agent asks duration first, or routes to confirm_service without asking the gender gate.
